qt: Gouraud shading is the current de jure shading standard in accelerated ------------ hardware.
When we need an object to "glow" in a scene, we can do this with ------------ light.
As f represents the attenuation of light, then f only approaches zero, when d approaches at distance -------------.
Light is mostly reflected in case of ___________ surfaces.
One problem with Gouraud shading is that the ----------- intensities can never be greater than the intensities at the edges.
Refractive index is a function of temperature, mostly due to density changes in materials with changes in temperature.

In order to get a more realistic representation of lighting, we'll need to understand how light passes through a medium and how hitting the boundary layer at the ----------------- of two media can affect light's properties.

The attenuation formula is f =------------------------, where C, L and Q are the constand, linear and quadratic attenuation factors and d is the distance between the vertex being lit and the light source.
When obtaining normals for a triangle, which of the following mathematical constructs is NOT used?

In the forms of texture mapping, Image to world space and world space to image, each suffers from different problems related to minification and magnification.  Which of the two shows the following problem: When the texture is larger than the screen space it maps to, many texture units (texels) are never sampled?
The traditional approach in real-time computer graphics has been to calculate lighting at a vertex as a sum of the ________ light.
There are not many different ways of representing the intensity of a particular colour element.

The reflection coefficients are in the ------------------------- range and are specified as part of the material property. However, they are strictly empirical and since they simply adjust the overall intensity of the material colour, the material colour values are usually adjusted so the colour intensity varies rather than using a reflection coefficient.
----------- reflection is the effect of reflecting light toward the direction from which it came, no matter the orientation of the surface.

Unlike ambient light, the intensity of diffuse light is directional and is a function of the angle of the incoming light and the surface. This type of shading is called Lambertian shading after Lambert's _________law.
We want our scene to look more realistic, we should use _________ lights.

The Phong reflection model simplifies light-matter interactions essentially into 4 vectors and a number of constants.  Each piece of the Phong model uses different vectors and constants.  Which portion does NOT include taking a dot product?
Dark lights are nothing more than lights in which one or more of the color values are_______________.
______________ is simply the calculation of color reflected by the surface.
The physical range of colours a device can display is called _____________________ of the device.
___________ light is reflected in all directions from surface.

When you hit a surface in ray tracing, generally shadow rays are tested against all objects in a scene.  If these rays come back saying they hit an object in the scene, which of the following do you do?
op: add only the ambient light from that light source to the object
add all components (i.e. ambient, diffuse and specular) from that light source to the object.
add none of the light from that light source to the object
add all EXCEPT the ambient light from that light source to the object (i.e. diffuse and specular)

qt: If we are not worried about computational resources, we should use _____________ lights.
Light in the real worlds loses its --------- as the inverse square of the distance.
An algorithm that clips a polygon must deal with many ----------------- cases. The case is particularly note worthy in that the concave polygon is clipped into ----------- isolate polygons.
Loosely, the alpha component of the RGBA quad represents the _______________of a surface.
_______________ colors specified for objects that generate light themselves.

Projection can be defined as a mapping of point P(x, y, z) onto its image P`(x`, y`, z` ) in the ----------------, which constitutes the display surface. The mapping is determined by a projection line called the projector that passes through P and intersects the ----------------.

What makes this really challenging to model is that the index of refraction for most materials is a function of the ------------------- of the light. This means that not only is there a shift in the angle of refraction, but that the shift is different for differing --------------- of light.
There are _________ angles associated with spotlights.
The rate at which light intensity decreases at a greater distance is called as ---------------------------.
The ColorSpace tool is a handy tool that we can use to interactively add two colours together to see the effects of the various strategies for handling oversaturated colours.
The reflected light wave turns out to be a --------------------- case since light is reflected at the same angle as the incident wave when the surface is smooth and uniform, as we'll assume for now.
Length L depends on the angle alpha and the z coordinate of the point to be projected and L can be represented by --------------------.

Consider the following problem from lighting: A point (P1) is at (0, 0, 0) with normal equal to 1/(2*sqrt(2))*(sqrt(2), 2, sqrt(2)).  The light is located at (0, 1, 0).  The viewer is located at (1, 1, 1).  The specular and diffuse coefficients for the light are both (1, 1, 1), with the ambient portion as (.2, .2, .2).  Complete the reflection vector.
		[ ------------]
r = 		[ 0 ]
		[1/sqrt(2)]
Choose from the values below:
Lambertian shading was used mostly back when computers weren't fast enough to do ________ in real time.
___________ lighting is not dependent on any source.
We have limited computational resources, we should use _____________ lights.
Which is the oldest in given types of the shading?

Refractive index is a function of temperature, mostly due to changes in ---------------------- of materials with changes in temperature. A simple correction can be applied in most circumstances to allow us to use a value given at one temperature at another.
_________ lights should be avoided because they are not for real time environment.

The Phong reflection model simplifies light-matter interactions essentially into 4 vectors and a number of constants. Which piece of the Phong model is responsible for giving spheres their bright white spots?
A polygon is usually defined by a sequence of vertices and ------------------
In the Phong reflection model, there are 3 constants (a, b, c) which are used to describe the qualities of which of the following phenomena?
op: The amount to perturb reflection vectors as they are mirrored across the normal
The attenuation of a point light source with distance
The material reaction to ambient, diffuse and specular light respectively
The size (in each dimension) which the light is assumed to have

qt: _____________ projection is obtained by projecting points along parallel lines that are not perpendicular to projection plane.

Ambient light is the light that comes from ----------------------directions, thus all surfaces are illuminated equally regardless of orientation. However, this is a big hack in traditional lighting calculations since "real" ambient light really comes from the light reflected from the "environment."
If we want any object to glow, we should use ________________ lights.

The value returned is a unique small integer identifier for the window. The range of allocated identifiers starts at --------------------. This window identifier can be used when calling glutSetWindow.
A parametric curve is one whose defining equations are given in terms of a -------------, common, independent variable called the parametric variable.
We allow the parametric variable to take on values only in the interval ----------------.
The reflected light wave turns out to be a / an  ______________ case since light is reflected at the same angle as the incident wave (when the surface is smooth and uniform, as we'll assume for now).
A space curve can be confined to a plane.

Signed integer color components, when specified, are linearly mapped to floating-point values such that the most positive representable value maps to 1.0, and the most negative representable value maps to ------------------. Floating-point values are mapped directly.

End points and an intermediate point on the curve, then we now --------------------- quantities that we can express in terms of these coefficients (3 points x 3 coordinates each), and we can use these three points to define a unique curve.
--------- is the change in the direction of a light ray when it bounces off the boundary between two media.
The ________________ functions multiply the current matrix by a rotation matrix.
Normalized cross product of two vectors on that surface provides real number.

Imagine a curve in three-dimensional space, each point on the curve has a unique set of coordinates: a specific x value, y value, and z value. Each coordinate is controlled by a -------------- parametric equation.
Normalized cross product of two vectors on that surface provides normal vector
_________________ sets the global idle call back to be 'func' so a GLUT program can perform background processing tasks or continuous animation when window system events are not being received.
While building polygonal model of surfaces, we should use T-intersections.
Brenstien polynomial functions are the basic functions of ______________ curves.
A tangent vector certainly defines the slope at one end of the curve, but a vector has characteristics of......
the French mathematician who made significant contributions to our understanding of cubic and quadratic polynomials.
The degree of a Bezier curve is equal to n-1, where n is the number of control points

With similar expressions for y(u) and z(u). Again the a, b, c and d terms are constant coefficients. As we did with Equation for a plane curve, we combine the x(u), y(u) , and z(u) expressions into a single vector equation P(u) = ---------------------------------------.
To ensure a smooth transition from one section of a piecewise __________ to the next, we can impose various continuity conditions at the connection points
The glClearColor function specifies the red, green, blue, and alpha values used by glClear to clear the color buffers. Values specified by glClearColor are clamped to the range ------------------.
Another way to define a space curve by using intermediate points and the tangents at each end for making the curve
Bezier curve is tangent to the lines connecting _____________.
op: Fist two points and last two point

qt: OpenGL is well structured with an intuitive design and logical commands. Efficient OpenGL routines typically result in applications with fewer lines of code than those that make up programs generated using other graphics libraries or packages. In addition, OpenGL drivers --------------- information about the underlying hardware, freeing the application developer from having to design for specific hardware features.

If we assign a different value to the parametric variable for the intermediate point, then we obtain different values for the coefficients. This, in turn, means that a different curve is produced, although it passes through the -------------- three points.
A space curve is not confined to a plane. It is free to twist through space. To define a space curve we must use parametric functions that are ----------------------.

The mathematics of parametric equation is the basis for ------------- curves.
glutReshapeWindow requests a change in the size of the current window. The width and height parameters are size extents in pixels. The width and height must be ---------------- values.

The matrix generated by gluPerspective is multiplied by the current matrix, just as if glMultMatrix were called with the generated matrix. To load the perspective matrix onto the current matrix stack instead, precede the call to gluPerspective with a call to -----------------------.

Using a lighting model based upon the Blinn Phong model means that we'll always get a uniform specular highlight based upon the colour of the --------------- light and material, which means that all reflections based on this model, will be reminiscent of plastic.
Bit mask to select a window with multisampling support. If multisampling is not available, a ----------------- window will automatically be chosen.

An independent consortium, the OpenGL Architecture Review Board, guides the OpenGL specification. With broad industry support, OpenGL is the only truly open, vendor-neutral, --------------- graphics standard.

When you hit a surface in ray tracing, generally shadow rays are tested against all objects in a scene.  If these rays come back saying they hit an object in the scene, which of the following do you do?
op: add only the ambient light from that light source to the object
add all EXCEPT the ambient light from that light source to the object (i.e. diffuse and specular)
add all components (i.e. ambient, diffuse and specular) from that light source to the object.
